name: best64.rule
purpose: The 64 most effective base mutations (case, appending digits/years, leet, reversal). Best coverage-to-speed ratio - the first rule for any cracking job.
whenToUse: First pass over rockyou: maximum hits for minimum time before you reach for heavy rules.

name: rockyou-30000.rule
purpose: ~30,000 rules derived from analyzing the RockYou leak. A good mid-size set: much wider than best64 but still fast.
whenToUse: Second pass after best64 when you want more coverage without moving to huge rule sets.

name: dive.rule
purpose: One of the largest built-in sets (~99,000 rules) - very deep mutations. High coverage at the cost of time.
whenToUse: When fast rules are exhausted, you have time/GPU, and want to squeeze the most out of a given wordlist.

name: d3ad0ne.rule
purpose: Popular set (~34,000 rules) by d3ad0ne - balanced mutations that catch human password variations well.
whenToUse: An alternative/complement to dive: run it when best64 and rockyou-30000 didn't finish the hashes.

name: T0XlC.rule / T0XlC-insert_*.rule
purpose: The T0XlC set (~11,000 rules) and its insert variants (inserting symbols/digits at various positions). Good against passwords with special chars inside the word.
whenToUse: When you suspect inserts of special chars/digits not only at the end but inside the word.

name: generated2.rule
purpose: A machine-generated set (~65,000 rules) - broad mutation coverage. Heavy but effective on large hash lists.
whenToUse: A deep pass when you need maximum variation and have the compute budget.

name: leetspeak.rule
purpose: A small set of leet substitutions (a->4, e->3, o->0, s->$, etc.). Targets leet variations of dictionary words.
whenToUse: When passwords are words with leet substitutions; cheap to add to any wordlist.

name: toggles1-5.rule
purpose: Toggle-case across the first N positions: toggles1 - 1 position, ... toggles5 - up to 5. For cases like Password vs pAssword vs PASSword.
whenToUse: When hashes are case-sensitive but your candidates are lowercase; layer on top of a wordlist.
size: toggles1 ... toggles5 (grows with the number of positions)

name: InsidePro-PasswordsPro.rule / InsidePro-HashManager.rule
purpose: Large sets from InsidePro (PasswordsPro / HashManager). Broad mutations that perform well on real leaks.
whenToUse: As a heavy alternative to dive/generated2 for a deep pass.

name: OneRuleToRuleThemAll.rule
purpose: The famous set (~52,000 rules) by NotSoSecure/stealthsploit: assembled from the best public rules. One of the most effective universal sets.
whenToUse: When you want one strong universal rule instead of cycling through several. NOT in stock Kali - download from GitHub.

name: OneRuleToRuleThemStill.rule
purpose: The updated/optimized successor to OneRuleToRuleThemAll: fewer rules at comparable coverage - faster on slow hashes.
whenToUse: When One Rule is too heavy for a slow algorithm (bcrypt, etc.) but you still want its coverage.

name: Kaonashi.rule
purpose: Rules from the Kaonashi project (research into real-world passwords): strong human-like mutations that complement One Rule well.
whenToUse: For a deep pass on real leaks; download from GitHub (not in stock Kali).

name: How to apply rules (syntax)
purpose: Rules (-r) combine with a wordlist in dictionary-attack mode (-a 0). You can chain several -r flags - they apply sequentially (a cartesian product of mutations).
whenToUse: Cheat sheet for running any rule above.
